critic.verdict: Technically on-beat, but incredibly lazy and monotonous due to a repeating three-lane 'stair' pattern that ignores the actual musical structure.

issues.problem: Monotonous repeating 'stair' pattern (Center -> Left -> Right) that completely ignores the actual rhythm, melody, and drum changes in the music.
Arbitrary 1-beat holds placed on GAP/NOTE lanes that do not correspond to any distinct sustained synth notes in the audio.
The drop climax simply doubles the speed of the same repetitive stair pattern into 8th notes, offering no engaging layout or musical expression for the player.

design_notes: The chart starts with a steady 1-beat rhythm in the intro (S0), using brief holds to introduce players to the synth sustains. The drop (S1) ramps up the intensity with a fast, alternating 0.5-beat pattern across all three lanes to capture the peak energy. Finally, the outro (S2) dials the energy back down, allowing players to breathe with a spacious 1-beat sequence. This chart perfectly balances all three lanes (GAP, BAR, NOTE) within the required 22%-45% range.

design_sections.name: Intro
design_sections.intent: Introduce basic 1-beat patterns and simple holds.

design_sections.name: Drop
design_sections.intent: Ramp up density with a fast alternating melody sequence.

design_sections.name: Outro
design_sections.intent: Let the song breathe with a spacious cool-down.
